texto: Much better now. And Sunny—there's no such thing as 'too much' when it comes to us. Too much mutual interest, or awareness, or desire, or too much damn love—there's no such thing.""I love you beyond obsession," I murmur, terrified because it is too much."I love you to insanity," he replies, utterly solemn. "That's who I am, and that is who we are. I don't have any qualms about admitting it, and I don't have any regrets. Do you?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Olivia Lynde que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Much better now. And Sunny—there's no such thing as 'too much' when it comes to us. Too much mutu…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, love, obsession, com foco em obsession e too-much.

frase_original: Much better now. And Sunny—there's no such thing as 'too much' when it comes to us. Too much mutual interest, or awareness, or desire, or too much damn love—there's no such thing.""I love you beyond obsession," I murmur, terrified because it is too much."I love you to insanity," he replies, utterly solemn. "That's who I am, and that is who we are. I don't have any qualms about admitting it, and I don't have any regrets. Do you?

texto: I may have survived, but I am broken," I remind him sadly."No, Sunny, you're just a bit battered. We both are. Or if we're broken, that's only when we're alone. But now we're together, baby! And together, we're both whole.
explicacao: Citação de Olivia Lynde sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “I may have survived, but I am broken," I remind him sadly."No, Sunny, you're just a bit battered.…” articula reflexao, broken, completeness em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como completeness e love.
